items.summary: Gold prices are falling as investors, facing the backdrop of a worsening Iran conflict, take shelter in the dollar.

items.headline: Treasury yields climb higher as investors monitor Iran war and soaring oil price
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields climbed higher on Monday as oil prices soared past $100 a barrel and increased inflation fears among investors.

items.headline: Telecom stocks have had a great start to the year — and they’re still quite cheap
items.summary: Several companies in the S&P 500’s communications sector trade at low price-to-earnings valuations, with attractive dividend yields well supported by cash flow.

items.headline: The cost of Germany’s long and painful nuclear regret
items.summary: An energy analysis published by JPMorgan has put some numbers on the extent of that error that German Chancellor Friedrich Merz has admitted from its decision to shutter nuclear production in wake of the accident at Japan’s Fukushima plant in 2011.

items.headline: One year after Trump’s sovereignty threats, Canadians keep ‘elbows up’
items.summary: An unusual swell of Canadian patriotism seen after Trump's threats and tariffs last year has evolved into a new social and economic order.

items.headline: ‘Sky is the limit’: Analysts warn oil prices could surge further
items.summary: Energy analysts warned that there was no limit for oil prices as they rose in response to the war in the Middle East.

items.headline: Iran's strategic oil island thrust into the spotlight as Middle East conflict escalates
items.summary: Kharg Island serves as the centerpiece for Iran's oil industry, accounting for roughly 90% of the country’s crude exports.

items.headline: G7 scrambles emergency meeting and could deploy unprecedented oil reserves as prices soar: report
items.summary: The finance ministers are reportedly considering making 300 million to 400 million barrels available.

items.headline: Hims & Hers stock rockets on reported end to Novo Nordisk weight-loss drug dispute
items.summary: Hims & Hers shares jumped nearly 50% in premarket trade on Monday after a report it has ended a dispute with Novo Nordisk by striking a partnership with the Danish drug giant.

items.headline: Congressional Democrats demand reversal of Russian oil sales into India as energy prices soar

items.headline: How JPMorgan became the latest Wall Street firm to have its research in Scott Bessent’s crosshairs
items.summary: JPMorgan thinks the DFC cannot offer adequate insurance to all the vessels presently hoping to transit the Strait of Hormuz without a change in legislation from Congress. Bessent disagrees.

items.headline: Why Morgan Stanley says U.S. outperformance will continue, even if normalcy returns
items.summary: U.S. stocks have been outperforming international rivals since the Iran conflict began, since the U.S. is a net exporter of oil while major European and Asian competitors require imports.

items.headline: Why China can withstand oil's surge past $100 more easily than other countries

items.headline: European markets fall over 2% on Middle East turmoil as oil price surges

items.headline: China consumer inflation hits three-year high as producer deflation eases
